subject: Mozi Minute: Contrast Closing
body:
Words I like: Nothing is ever expensive in isolation, only in relation. But the key to sales is comparing your price to the right other number.

Mozi Minute: The Contrast Close

They've proven that people make decisions based on relative value, not absolute value. Yet most businesses present prices in isolation. Here's how to use contrast to make your prices feel smaller.

The framework:

- Start with the cost of their problem (yearly)

Ex: Customer losing $300k/year on bad ad spend
- Break that into monthly cost

Ex: That's $25k/month in waste
- Present your solution cost (monthly)

Ex: Our service: $5k/month
- Show the contrast ratio

Contrast ratio: Every $1 spent saves $5

TLDR: Anchor them high with their problem cost first. Then hit them with the contrast. Frame it as a black and white return.

Pro tip: People would rather avoid loss than acquire gain. Framing solutions to make known problems go away can be more compelling (and believable) than anchoring on unknown upside. If you're struggling to close, try this and you may get more sales across the line.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Want more proven sales tactics? We have open spots for our next Scaling Workshop in Vegas on April 7/8th.

Frame first, close more,

Alex

PS - Your sales team's reaction when you roll this out…
